entities.name: MYO-INOSITOL
entities.description: An abundant form of inositol that serves as a substrate to diverse biosynthesis pathways across life forms.

entities.name: MYO-INOSITOL OXIDATION PATHWAY
entities.description: A metabolic pathway involving the oxidation of myo-inositol, potentially linked to stress resilience in plants.

entities.name: STRESS RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to withstand or recover from adverse environmental conditions.

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Multicellular eukaryotes in which the myo-inositol oxidation pathway and MIOX enzymes are studied.

entities.name: BIOSYNTHESIS PATHWAYS
entities.description: Biological processes that produce complex molecules from simpler ones, including those for myo-inositol.

entities.name: PLANT PHYSIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the internal functions and processes of plants, influenced by the MI pathway.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing the genetic makeup of crops for better yield or resilience.

entities.name: MYO-INOSITOL OXYGENASE
entities.description: An enzyme (MIOX) that catabolizes myo-inositol into D-glucuronic acid.

entities.name: D-GLUCURONIC ACID
entities.description: The product of myo-inositol catabolism by the MIOX enzyme.

entities.name: STRESS RESPONSIVE PATHWAYS
entities.description: Metabolic networks that are activated or modified in response to environmental stress.

entities.name: STRESS CONDITION
entities.description: Environmental factors that trigger the upregulation of MI metabolic pathway genes.

entities.name: ARABIDOPSIS
entities.description: A model plant species used to study the differential expression of MIOX genes.

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A cereal crop used to study the differential expression of MIOX genes.

entities.name: MIOX PROTEINS
entities.description: Proteins encoded by MIOX genes involved in the myo-inositol oxidation pathway.

relationships.description: Engineering this pathway can increase stress resilience in plants
The pathway is present and studied in various plant species
Serves as the substrate for the oxidation pathway
Differentially expressed according to developmental and stress signals
Differentially expressed according to developmental and stress signals
Holds potential implications for crop improvement
Crosstalks with other important stress responsive pathways in plants
